patch.value: pm cal --include events|scheduled expands recurring events without default cap

When using --include events (or the new --include scheduled alias) without --occurrence-limit or --to, pm calendar expands recurring events across the full lookahead window (default 365 days). A weekly recurring event generates 52+ calendar rows. Agents calling this without bounds get unexpectedly large output. Should apply a sensible default cap (e.g., 4 weeks / 28 days) for the events-only filter, or warn when expansion exceeds a threshold.

author: cursor-agent
message: Start: add default recurring event cap for events-only calendar filter

value.note: apply DEFAULT_EVENTS_ONLY_LOOKAHEAD_DAYS=28 cap when --include events-only without explicit bounds

value.author: cursor-agent
value.text: Fix applied: when --include resolves to events-only (no deadlines/reminders) and no explicit --to, --occurrence-limit, or --recurrence-lookahead-days is set, the recurrence lookahead now defaults to 28 days instead of 365. A warning 'recurring_events_default_cap_applied:28d' is added to the result so agents/users know the cap was applied and can override with explicit flags. When any explicit bound is provided, the full 365-day default is preserved. Tests: 14/14 calendar-command.spec.ts passed. Build: clean.

author: cursor-agent

patch.value: Events-only calendar filter now defaults to 28-day lookahead instead of 365 when no explicit bounds are set. Warning is emitted. Existing explicit-bound usage is unchanged. 14/14 tests pass.

message: Close: recurring event default cap applied for events-only filter

patch.value: Applied DEFAULT_EVENTS_ONLY_LOOKAHEAD_DAYS=28 cap for events-only calendar filter without explicit bounds
Events-only calendar filter uses bounded 28-day lookahead by default; explicit bounds override
28-day cap applied; warning emitted; explicit --to/--occurrence-limit/--recurrence-lookahead-days preserves full 365-day default
